expansion: Spine-Runner (the minimal frame-walking conductor agent that walks a v2 frame-spine plan step by step)
Customer Experience Gate (the final validation step verifying the implementation achieves the intended customer experience)

decode: prosecution = finding pass; defense = rebuttal pass; judge = scoring pass; see skills/adversarial-review/SKILL.md

expansion: frame (the structured plan skeleton) / frame-spine (the machine-readable <!-- frame-spine --> block that routes plan execution to specialist agents)
frame slice (a single executable plan step identified as sN, carrying the <!-- frame-slice --> HTML comment block with step_id: s{N} as a YAML field inside)
frame port (a named delivery point in the frame-spine, e.g. implement-test, implement-docs, review, release-hygiene)
adapter (a skill configured to execute a specific port's work) / skill-as-adapter (the pattern of loading a skill as the work specification for a frame port)
decode: See Documents/Design/frame-architecture.md § Adapter Model and CLAUDE.md § Senior Engineer + skill-as-adapter pattern

expansion: Session Memory Contract rule NN (e.g. SMC-01, SMC-08)
decode: SMC-NN = Session Memory Contract rule NN; the full numbered list is in skills/session-memory-contract/SKILL.md

expansion: plan-issue-{ID} (the <!-- plan-issue-{ID} --> durable HTML comment posted on the GitHub issue carrying the approved implementation plan)
engagement-record-{phase}-{ID} (the durable decision-record comment, e.g. <!-- engagement-record-plan-732 -->, recording load-bearing decisions for a pipeline phase)
engagement gate (a required methodology checkpoint the agent must fire via AskUserQuestion — auto-mode does not suppress it)

replacement: worth-it check (the upstream assessment of whether a feature is worth building — bet / falsifier / alternative)

expansion: upstream-onboarding (the orientation brief and standards check an agent runs when resuming an issue from a prior pipeline phase)
scope classification (the Code-Conductor tier decision: full pipeline vs abbreviated pipeline, based on a 5-criterion rubric)
intent routing (natural-language routing of user requests to pipeline commands) / nl_intent_routing (the JSON key governing routing in routing-config.json)
routing-config.json (the canonical routing table at skills/routing-tables/assets/routing-config.json governing intent routing and specialist dispatch)

credits[] (the pipeline credit array field in the <!-- pipeline-metrics --> block) / pipeline-metrics block (the <!-- pipeline-metrics --> HTML comment in PR bodies recording what each phase produced)
credit provenance (the source of a pipeline credit — which adapter produced it) / witness type (how the credit was verified, e.g. validation-evidence, manual, auto)
enforce verdict (the pass/fail judgment on a pipeline credit's compliance check) / enforce check (the automated test a credit must pass to be considered compliant)
judge-rulings YAML block (the <!-- judge-rulings --> HTML comment in PR review comments carrying the machine-readable adversarial review verdict)
review-judge-produced-{PR} (the <!-- review-judge-produced-{PR} --> sentinel comment marking that a judge verdict exists for the given PR number)
BDD (Behavior-Driven Development) / behavioral scenario (a Given/When/Then scenario that verifies a customer outcome)
decode: BDD framework detection and scenario wiring: see skills/bdd-scenarios/SKILL.md

expansion: prosecution depth (the calibration metric controlling how deeply the adversarial prosecution examines each change category — transitions between skip, light, and full examination depth; see skills/calibration-pipeline/SKILL.md)
calibration fixture (a known-good or known-bad test case verifying the pipeline metrics schema) / calibration pipeline (the end-to-end infrastructure for verifying PR credit emission)
decode: See skills/calibration-pipeline/ for the full reference set

replacement: time-sensitive test (a test whose behavior depends on when it runs, such as date-based logic or elapsed-time checks)
decode: now-coupled = assertion tightly bound to current date; wall-clock dependent = broader category; surfaces in issue tracker (e.g. #723)

expansion: D1 / D2 / D3 (auto-mode boundary rules governing which tool calls auto-approve, which prompt the user, and when AskUserQuestion is unconditional)
decode: D1 / D2 / D3 = auto-mode boundary rules; see CLAUDE.md § Auto-mode boundary for the full definitions

expansion: session-startup (the plugin-distributed SessionStart hook that runs the cleanup detector and plugin drift check at each conversation start)

decode: worktree = parallel git working tree; sibling = live peer worktree; orphan = branch with no live worktree and no open PR; see skills/session-startup/SKILL.md

expansion: project references (named links from agent context to long-lived project documents) / sidecar (the companion .ref.json file alongside a doc that declares when it should load)

plugin cache (the installed plugin version snapshot at ~/.claude/plugins/cache, keyed by version) / plugin.json (the version manifest in .claude-plugin/plugin.json that triggers cache invalidation when the version field changes)
proxy prosecution (the prosecution pass run against an external GitHub code review, treating the reviewer's comments as prosecution findings)
inline-dispatch (running a frame slice's adapter in the active conductor context via executor: inline, rather than delegating to a separate specialist Agent call)
halt-return (the structured YAML block Senior Engineer returns when it cannot safely complete a slice, signaling Code-Conductor to intervene rather than producing partial work)

subagent-env-handshake (the <!-- subagent-env-handshake v1 --> block prepended to each specialist Agent dispatch, carrying parent HEAD/branch/CWD/dirty-fingerprint for working-tree consistency verification)
persist-changes (the git-portable commit+push primitive in skills/persist-changes/SKILL.md, caller-parameterized, executed at the terminal implementation step)

decode: post-PR review = checklist run after PR merges; post-merge cleanup = worktree removal, orphan branch deletion, tracking file archival; see skills/post-pr-review/SKILL.md
